hypothesis: If GFN1-xTB band gaps for MOFs are used as a physically informed baseline and an XGBoost Δ-learning model trained on ST-135, RAC, and APRDF descriptors is fit to predict the xTB-to-DFT correction, then cross-validated predictions of PBE and HSE06 band gaps will have lower error than both direct descriptor-based models and fine-tuned MOFTransformer/CGCNN baselines because Δ-learning captures and corrects systematic biases in the semi-empirical method using chemically informative features.
